Title: The ethylene response factor ERF1A regulates UV-C-induced delayed ripening in peach fruit

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel; induced by UV-C; reduced in ERF1A-silenced peel
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a putative ERF1A target positively associated with volatile balance during ripening; reduced upon ERF1A silencing
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter tested in GUS assay; CaMV35S used to overexpress ERF1A in protoplast transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ERF1A RNAi followed by RT-qPCR; promoter transactivation GUS reporter assay in Arabidopsis protoplasts; volatile profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering peach aroma/volatile composition together with ERF1A
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCXE11 was identified as a putative ERF1A target based on promoter binding-site analysis. 2. Its expression decreased in ERF1A-silenced peel during UV-C-associated ripening. 3. ERF1A activated the PpCXE11 promoter in GUS assays, linking it to volatile regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats PpCXE11 as a downstream ERF1A target rather than an independently dissected primary gene.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel; induced by UV-C at transcript level; transcript and protein reduced in ERF1A-silenced peel
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with ERF1A-mediated volatile regulation; reduced expression/protein in silenced fruit accompanies altered aldehydes/esters
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter tested in GUS assay; CaMV35S used to overexpress ERF1A in protoplast transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ERF1A RNAi; proteomics; RT-qPCR; GUS reporter assay; volatile profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target for modifying peach aroma under postharvest UV-C or ripening control strategies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCXE13 emerged as a strong putative ERF1A target from promoter and proteogenomic analyses. 2. ERF1A silencing lowered both PpCXE13 transcript and protein abundance. 3. ERF1A activated the PpCXE13 promoter, supporting a role in volatile balance regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a downstream target, not the main experimentally centered gene.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel; UV-C-upregulated at transcript level; reduced transcript/protein abundance in ERF1A-silenced peel
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive ERF1A target linked to SA signaling; ERF1A silencing reduces PpSABP2 and is associated with elevated SA levels
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter tested in GUS assay; CaMV35S used to overexpress ERF1A in protoplast transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ERF1A RNAi; RT-qPCR; proteomics; GUS reporter assay; hormone quantification
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream node for tuning SA-linked ripening/stress tradeoffs in postharvest peach
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpSABP2 was identified as a putative ERF1A target and associated with hormone signaling. 2. ERF1A silencing reduced PpSABP2 transcript and related protein abundance while SA levels increased. 3. ERF1A activated the PpSABP2 promoter in protoplast assays.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A downstream target gene highlighted by functional promoter activation, but secondary to ERF1A in experimental focus.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in peel 8 h after UV-C; much less emphasized in flesh; strongly induced in Morsiani 90 peel after UV-C
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter for ERF1A overexpression construct in protoplast transactivation assay; CaMV35S promoter in pCambia hairpin RNAi construct
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Ethylene; ripening-associated sugars, phenolics/anthocyanins, salicylic acid, ABA, auxin, and volatiles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel primarily; whole fruit ripening traits also assessed
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Delays ripening while modulating quality-related metabolites such as sugars, phenolics, anthocyanins, and volatiles
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ripening-associated hormone signaling, volatile metabolism, phenolic/flavonoid metabolism, sugar metabolism, and cell wall remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PpCXE11; PpCXE13; PpSABP2; PpACO11; PpCLSE1; PpFUT1; PpGDSL3; PpMYB4; PpWRKY40; PpHIDH; PpCYP450; PpFMO; PpPIP2; PpCEP14; PpAATP
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of downstream target genes; positive control of UV-C-delayed ripening network
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PpCXE11, PpCXE13, and PpSABP2 promoters; ERF1A silencing decreases expression of multiple ripening-related targets including PpACO11, PpCLSE1, PpFUT1, PpGDSL3, PpMYB4, PpWRKY40, PpHIDH, and PpCYP450
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes UV-C-induced delayed ripening by sustaining ethylene-associated signaling, firmness, reduced water loss, coloration, and coordinated sugar/phenolic/volatile metabolism; silencing accelerates ripening and softening
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central regulator and key integrator of UV-C-induced ripening delay in peach peel
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peel-inducible by UV-C in peach fruit
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-C treatment; room-temperature ripening; post-cold ripening; ERF1A RNAi silencing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; transient RNAi silencing by vacuum agroinfiltration; metabolite profiling; hormone assays; volatile analysis; proteomics; promoter GUS transactivation assay; microscopy/immunolocalization
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Global peel DNA 5mC and RNA m6A increased after UV-C, but targeted bisulfite sequencing showed no cytosine methylation changes in ERF1A and coding-sequence analysis detected no UV-C-induced mutations
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1180494; proteomics, PRIDE PXD060506; metabolomics (GC-MS/LC-MS), accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising postharvest and breeding target to delay peach ripening and maintain quality; could be combined with downstream targets controlling firmness, hormone balance, and aroma, but context dependence under non-UV-C conditions should be evaluated
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes accelerated softening, increased water loss, altered hormone homeostasis, reduced desirable pigments/metabolites, and volatile shifts; overmanipulation may affect aroma and normal ripening progression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study explicitly identifies ERF1A as the main experimentally interrogated regulator. Effects were most evident in peel, the tissue directly exposed to UV-C. Functional relevance under physiological non-UV-C conditions remains unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UV-C strongly induced PpERF1A in peach peel, making it the most responsive AP2/ERF transcription factor detected. 2. Transient RNAi silencing of PpERF1A reduced ethylene production, accelerated softening and ripening, increased water loss, and altered peel cell-wall epitopes. 3. ERF1A silencing reprogrammed sugars, phenolics, volatiles, and hormones, increasing auxin and SA while decreasing ABA. 4. Proteomics and expression analyses identified multiple ripening-related downstream genes, with direct promoter activation validated for PpCXE11, PpCXE13, and PpSABP2.
